V1.name: Fundamentals Trajectory
V1.rationale: **DOWNGRADED Apr 3 (5→4, council concession).** [COUNCIL: all, o3, 2026-04-03] Council scored 4/5 — conceded after adversarial review. Rubric 5 requires "Business inflecting meaningfully upward" but FY2026 revenue is guided DOWN 3-8% (€740-780M vs €803M). Order book declined 342→288 units. The inflection is forward-looking (FID pipeline, Q1 ordering pace), not current. Leading indicators are exceptional: 84 mtpa FID record, 14 LNGC orders YTD 2026, Ras Laffan structural catalyst (17% capacity destroyed, 3-5yr repair), force majeure on long-term contracts. But the trough IS happening now — rubric says "inflecting" (present tense). 4/5 is the honest score until reported numbers confirm re-acceleration (expected FY2027). *(Prior: 5/5 from Mar 20. Sources: FY2025 Earnings Call; Ras Laffan Impact Assessment; Council validation 2026-04-03)*

V2.name: Valuation Mismatch
V2.rationale: **UPGRADED Apr 7 (AVERAGE→STRONG, user override).** EV/EBITDA is the appropriate primary methodology for an asset-light, net-cash royalty business where P/E structurally understates value (net cash ~€300M drags earnings yield; royalty model has no capital-intensive denominator). PW FV €346 vs €203.60 = +70% mismatch (EV/EBITDA). Council o3 FV €309 = +52% (also STRONG territory). Council gpt-5.4 P/E-based FV €247 = +21% (AVERAGE territory) — methodologically disadvantaged for this capital structure. Multi-method stress test: weakest method (P/E at €247) sits at AVERAGE/STRONG boundary; disqualified as primary on capital-structure grounds, not ignored. **All-STRONG checkpoint (Apr 7): V2 flagged as most vulnerable dimension — two independent council runs scored AVERAGE. Retained at STRONG per user judgment; noted as skepticism point.** *(Prior: AVERAGE from Apr 4. Sources: Fair-value-estimation Mar 28; Council validations Apr 3-4)*

V3.name: Identifiable Mispricing Reason
V3.rationale: **MAINTAINED Apr 3 (council scored 4/5 — pushed back).** [COUNCIL: all, o3, 2026-04-03] Council scored 4/5 citing "one STRUCTURAL watch-item (competing membrane)." Pushed back: competing membrane is a V5/V7 competitive risk, not a V3 mispricing identification issue. The mispricing reason IS specific, verifiable, and demonstrably temporary. Market conflates 3 distinct layers: (a) temporary geopolitical premium (~3-4%), (b) structural LNG deficit premium (Ras Laffan 17% destroyed, 3-5yr repair), (c) dual chokepoint energy security premium (Bab el-Mandeb reactivated Mar 28). Force majeure on long-term contracts creates contractual forcing function. Self-correcting mechanism is arithmetic: replacement FIDs → vessel orders → GTT revenue. *(Sources: CHANGELOG Mar 31; Council validation 2026-04-03)*

V4.name: Downside Protection
V4.rationale: **UPGRADED 2026-03-31 (3→4).** Rubric 4: "Limited failure modes. Binary risks bounded but with some uncertainty. Floor exists and is reasonably hard." Bear case €222 exceeds current price (~€198-204) — even under FID disappointment + margin compression, the stock is undervalued. Net cash ~€300M. €8.94 dividend (4.5% yield) provides income floor. Dual chokepoint reduces ceasefire-unwind risk to -5-8% (previously -€6-8/share). Per A18 (LESSONS.md), scarcity-asset with contracted backlog provides structural floor distinct from generic cyclicals. Asset-light royalty model = no physical asset risk, no debt. FY2026 EBITDA guided €490-530M (65-68% margin) — margin resilience confirmed. Elogen drag bounded -€15-20M/yr (gigafactory halted). *(Sources: Fair-value-estimation Mar 28; Filings; Bear Case risk scenarios; Resolution Report Mar 19; LESSONS.md A18)*

V5.name: Thesis Simplicity
V5.rationale: **UPGRADED Apr 5 (AVERAGE→STRONG).** One genuine condition with real uncertainty: LNG structural demand continues — HOLDING (consensus 4.5% growth, 84 mtpa FID record, force majeure urgency). Monopoly position (2,466 patents, all yards licensed, no commercial-scale competitor) and FID conversion (75-90% historical base rate, Q1 pace validating) are not real thesis dependencies — monopoly has no credible challenger at commercial scale (5-8yr minimum), and conversion is a "how much" question, not an "if." Effectively 1 condition, firmly holding. *(Sources: Bull Case; QUESTIONS.md; Resolution Report Mar 19)*

V6.name: Upside Optionality
V6.rationale: **UPGRADED 2026-03-31 (4→5).** Rubric 5: "5+ independent ways to win. Base case is conservative; multiple scenarios deliver outsized returns." 5 independent paths to outperformance, 2+ showing early evidence: (1) FID conversion pace — Q1 ordering acceleration (45-50+ vessels annualized 180-200 vs. Clarksons 115); (2) dual chokepoint reactivation (Mar 28 — Houthi entered war, Cape handles BOTH diversions, +15-30 vessels); (3) ULEC ethane carriers (12 orders, 100% GTT share); (4) LNG fuel adoption (€7M→€65M, growing); (5) Allied naval coalition escort corridors highlighting LNGC scarcity. Base case conservative given Q1 ordering acceleration and force majeure urgency. *(Nakilat 25-vessel charter removed Apr 2 — 2024 deal already in backlog, not a 2026 catalyst. See NOTES.md correction.)* *(Sources: CHANGELOG Mar 31; Daily Intelligence Briefing Mar 31; Transcripts; Bull Case)*

V7.name: Evidence Quality
V7.rationale: **UPGRADED Apr 7 (STRONG→EXTREME).** Two of three DATA GAPs that drove the Apr 3 council downgrade are now LARGELY RESOLVED via deep research: (1) **Patent expiry: RESOLVED** — 15 patents mapped across 6 generations (1995-2023). Foundational 1960s patents expired 35+ years ago, market share unchanged. Current-gen (Mark III Flex, NO96 Super+) covered by post-2014 families expiring 2034-2043. Filing pace accelerating (55→68/yr, 2021-2025). Non-patent moat (60yr know-how, classification approvals, yard tooling, safety record) is the primary barrier. KFTC ruling bounded as latent yard leverage, not a cliff. (Source: Patent Portfolio report Apr 7) (2) **Royalty structure: RESOLVED** — definitively a flat rate per m² indexed to French labour cost (~€335/m² as of Oct 2015 base), NOT % of ship price. Volume discount for series orders. Council's "7% FV upside" from vessel-price-linked royalty DISCONFIRMED. (Source: Data Gap Analysis Apr 7 citing GTT investor presentations 2013-2016) Remaining DATA GAPs: (3) IPCEI €86M clawback — OPEN but bounded (€17M bank guarantee anchor; €17-86M range vs €347M cash; headline > financial risk; resolves URD late Apr); (4) Foam supply — OPEN but indirect (Dongsung FINETEC + Hankuk Carbon ~90% share; risk is shipyard-level revenue timing, not revenue loss). Core thesis rests on [FACT]-labeled data from 25 transcripts, audited filings, patent registry analysis, and investor presentation primary sources. RC 5/5. Remaining gaps are non-thesis-altering and bounded. *(Prior: STRONG from Apr 3 council. Sources: 25 transcripts; Filings; Patent Portfolio report Apr 7; Data Gap Analysis Apr 7; Council validation 2026-04-03)*

V8.name: Catalyst Clarity
V8.rationale: **UPGRADED 2026-03-31 (3→4).** Rubric 4: "Catalysts are specific and dated, with minor timing uncertainty." 3 dated catalysts within 4 months: (1) Q1 activity update Apr 21 — new CEO first presentation, order flow data; (2) EU Russian LNG ban Apr 25 — short-term contracts; (3) Q2 results Jul/Aug — first full-quarter post-dual-chokepoint. Dual chokepoint shipping repricing is near-term observable (insurance/freight for Bab el-Mandeb immediate). Minor timing uncertainty on whether catalysts trigger GTT-specific repricing vs. broader LNG sector move. *(Nakilat orders removed Apr 2 — 2024 deal, not a forward catalyst. See NOTES.md correction.)* *(Sources: CHANGELOG Mar 31; GTT IR calendar; Daily Intelligence Briefing Mar 31)*

blindspots: **Chinese yard indigenous technology (CSSC/Hudong)** — Opaque. No reliable English-language tracking of membrane tech development outside GTT licensing. Monitor via: CSSC annual reports (April), Lloyd's Register technology approvals (annual). Risk: a commercial delivery without GTT license would be thesis-altering.

**USTR port fee legislation timeline** — Fees proposed ($1.5M/call for Chinese-operated; $18/net ton for Chinese-built non-Chinese-owned) but **suspended Nov 10, 2025 – Nov 9, 2026** per US-China trade agreement. Currently no impact. Monitor USTR docket for post-Nov 2026 reinstatement. If reinstated, Chinese-built LNG carriers serving US trade face penalties → demand redistribution to Korean yards (positive for GTT).

**2025 FID cohort → order conversion rate** — 84 Mtpa sanctioned in 2025, but actual conversion to shipyard orders won't be visible until 2027-2028. Historical conversion: 75-90%. A sustained drop below 70% would signal structural change in project economics. No real-time tracking possible.

**Korean yard labor disputes** — Periodic strikes at HD KSOE/Hanwha can delay deliveries and shift ordering patterns. Monitor via: Korean business press. Usually short-lived (weeks) but can cause order bunching.

cyclical.context: Spikes >$100k signal urgency but revert. Current level unprecedented — sustained >$200K = structural not cyclical
cyclical.current: Post-Hormuz intelligence: ~$300K/day Atlantic & Pacific (Mar 27, +650% from pre-war). Note: Spark public platform shows $47.5K/$52K indicative — likely stale or pre-war cached data (subscription required for live rates). Verify via Spark subscription or broker

cyclical.context: Wide = positive for FIDs, but volatile QoQ. Tracks arb economics

cyclical.context: Lumpy by nature. Judge on rolling 4Q basis only. Annual >60 units = healthy
cyclical.current: **Q1 2026 OFFICIAL: 32 orders** (29 LNG carriers + 2 VLECs + 1 onshore 10,000 m³ tank) vs Q1 2025 16 orders = **DOUBLE YoY; 2nd best Q1 ever per CEO Michel**. LNGC annualized pace = **116/yr** (vs 60/yr tracking estimate Apr 6 — yard-level tracking undercounted by ~50% due to 0-4 week disclosure lag). Total commercial orders annualized = **128/yr**. Deliveries Q1: 22 LNGC + 1 FSRU + 2 LNG-as-fuel.
cyclical.name: Quarterly GTT order intake

noiseFilter: **Single-quarter freight rate spikes** — Hormuz disruption caused Spark25S to $161k/day (+163% in one day). These revert within weeks. Not indicative of structural ordering trends.
**Individual shipyard contract announcements** — Already priced; GTT confirms on ~1-month lag from yard announcement. The yard filing is lead; GTT press release is definitive.
**Short-term LNG price moves** — JKM/TTF moves <20% and <3 months are cyclical. Only sustained spread widening over 2+ quarters indicates structural arb shift.
**Analyst PT revisions** — GTT is well-covered; consensus moves reflect backward-looking models. Our edge is forward FID pipeline, not consensus tracking.
**FLNG/FSRU one-off orders** — Each is valuable but lumpy. Don't extrapolate one FLNG order into a trend.

CP2 Phase 2 FID ($8.6B, Mar 12). ME projects delayed (QatarEnergy NFW disrupted). Ras Laffan damage (17% / ~13 mtpa, 3-5yr repair) tightens long-term supply outlook.

QatarEnergy force majeure on LT LNG contracts; Ras Laffan repair **3-5 years** per QatarEnergy CEO (NOT months — Rystad confirms turbine delivery delays of 2-4 years).

trendLog.action: Logged — supports long-term thesis. No immediate action.
trendLog.assessment: Clarksons data confirms robust multi-year orderbook. 234 carriers may not be sufficient for 229 Mtpa capacity — supports continued ordering. Spark30S normalized to ~$52K/day from $278K spike.
trendLog.new: **234 newbuild LNG carriers** scheduled for delivery 2026-2030. 2026 is record delivery year. Fleet growth may lag 229 Mtpa new liquefaction capacity → structural shortfall possible.

trendLog.assessment: **Branch A (BULL) on 3/3 quantitative signals.** Order book inflected — first net quarterly rise since H1 2024. Thesis validation. Re-entry conditions 2 and 3 (ordering pace + guidance hold) MET. Rule 11 not triggered (no WATCHLIST/conviction/portfolio/scenario change — position MONITORING).
trendLog.new: **Official: 297 core units Mar 31 2026 (+9 net; first increase since H1 2024). Q1 orders: 32 total (29 LNGC + 2 VLEC + 1 onshore). FY2026 guidance reaffirmed €740-780M / €490-530M. CEO Michel: "2nd best Q1 commercial performance."** Annualized LNGC pace 116/yr — exceeds Clarksons 115 full-year forecast in a single quarter. Deliveries Q1: 22 LNGC + 1 FSRU + 2 LNG-as-fuel. Middle East: "no direct impact" (management non-attribution maintained). Petrobras 120-vessel weather-routing contract + HD Hyundai 102K m³ VLEC JDP = GTT Marine / new-IP optionality validation.
